region_of_origin: Thailand (manufactured by Thai President Foods PCL); widely consumed in Singapore
food_subgroup: Instant noodles — soup style, tom yum (sour-spicy) broth, fried noodle block with seasoning sachet

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not cover branded instant noodle products. Closest parent concept would be 'instant noodle food product' (FOODON:03315762) but no exact URI confirmed; null used to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Instant noodles — closest FoodEx2 category for dried/fried instant noodle products under 'Pasta and noodles'
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A039S (Noodles) used as closest defensible parent. No branded tom yum instant noodle code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for branded instant noodle products confirmed; null used to avoid fabrication.

preparation_method: prepared by boiling in water with seasoning sachets

serving.description: 1 full packet (prepared as tom yum soup noodles)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one full packet (90g dry weight), prepared by boiling the noodle block in water and adding all seasoning sachets to form a tom yum soup noodle dish.

nutrient_source.title: Mama Instant Noodles Tom Yum Shrimp — Thai President Foods PCL Singapore-sold product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP)
nutrient_source.organization: Thai President Foods PCL — Singapore-sold product NIP; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 2103689, Instant noodles, dry) for micronutrient plausibility
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Macronutrients, sodium, and key micronutrients taken directly from the Singapore-sold product NIP (Thai President Foods PCL). Micronutrient values (vitamins, minerals) cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central instant noodle entries for plausibility. All values are per 100g of prepared product (90g packet).

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Instant Noodles (soup style) — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~48 (low) sourced from University of Sydney GI Database for soup-style instant noodles. Mama Tom Yum Shrimp is not individually listed; the GI is an analogous estimate based on comparable soup-style instant noodle products in the database. GL per 90g serving calculated as (48 × available carbohydrate per 90g) / 100 ≈ 27.4, consistent with published GL for similar soup-style instant noodle products.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content — 1,050mg per 90g packet (52.5% of the 2,000mg/day recommended limit)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Mama Tom Yum noodles; consuming the full broth delivers the entire 1,050mg sodium load, and the sour-spicy tom yum flavour profile can mask perceived saltiness, leading to underestimation of sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~48 (low-medium) and GL ~27 per packet — moderate glycaemic impact per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI (~48) of soup-style instant noodles is favourable, but the glycaemic load of ~27 per full packet is moderate-to-high; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, avoid adding extra seasoning, and pair with non-starchy vegetables or protein to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1,050mg/packet), saturated fat from palm oil (5.04g/packet), and ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of Mama Tom Yum noodles due to the combination of high sodium (contributing to blood pressure elevation), saturated fat from palm oil (5.04g per packet), and the ultra-processed nature of the product, which is associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes in epidemiological studies.

shrimp_allergy.key_factor: Shrimp extract present in both flavour powder and potentially in the noodle block
shrimp_allergy.guidance: This product is contraindicated for individuals with crustacean shellfish allergy; shrimp extract is a declared ingredient in the seasoning sachet and cross-contamination risk exists in the noodle block manufacturing line.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mama Tom Yum Shrimp Instant Noodles (มาม่าต้มยำกุ้ง) is a Thai-origin instant noodle product manufactured by Thai President Foods PCL (Thailand) and widely sold in Singapore supermarkets (NTUC FairPrice, Giant) and Thai grocery stores. It has achieved cult status among Singapore's student and working population, particularly popularised by Thai restaurant workers, university students, and the vibrant Thailand-inspired street food culture. The product consists of a deep-fried wheat noodle block accompanied by a tom yum seasoning sachet containing shrimp extract, lemongrass, kaffir lime, galangal, and chilli. When prepared, the noodles are boiled and the seasoning is dissolved in the cooking water to form a sour-spicy-umami broth characteristic of Thai tom yum cuisine. The flavour profile — a complex balance of sour (lemongrass, kaffir lime), spicy (chilli), and umami (shrimp extract, galangal) — is considered by many Singapore consumers to be more complex and authentic-tasting than local instant noodle flavours. Nutritionally, one 90g packet provides approximately 385 kcal, 8.0g protein, 13.0g fat, and 57.0g carbohydrate. The sodium content is clinically significant at 1,050mg per packet (52.5% of the 2,000mg/day recommended limit), making it a high-sodium food of concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The GI is approximately 48 (low), but the glycaemic load per packet is approximately 27 (moderate-high), relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The shrimp allergen is a critical clinical concern — present in both the flavour powder and potentially in the noodle block itself. The product is certified halal and is dairy-free. It is classified as ultra-processed (NOVA Group 4).

confidence_reason: Macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore-sold product Nutrition Information Panel (Thai President Foods PCL). GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for soup-style instant noodles. All core fields are complete and supported by named approved sources.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported on the Singapore-sold product NIP and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific branded product as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content of the prepared product not reported on the NIP and not available in approved sources for this specific product. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product NIP (Thai President Foods PCL Singapore label). vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, and vitamin_d_mcg are 0.0 — confirmed zero or not detected per product NIP; these are naturally absent in a fried wheat noodle product with no vegetable or dairy components. GL per serving calculated as approximately 27.4 based on GI ~48 and available carbohydrate per 90g serving. Sodium per 90g serving = 1,050mg (1,167mg per 100g × 0.90). This product is classified NOVA Group 4 (ultra-processed). The tom yum broth acidity (lemongrass, kaffir lime) can mask perceived saltiness, a clinically relevant consideration for hypertension counselling. FoodEx2 code A039S used as closest defensible parent category for instant noodles; no branded tom yum instant noodle code exists. Q8 fix (iteration 3): aliases_by_language tamil array corrected to use exact verbatim strings matching the flat aliases array — 'மாமா டாம் யம் நூடுல்ஸ்' and 'மாமா டாம் யம் நூடுல்ஸ் (Māmā Ṭām Yam Nūṭuls)' — eliminating the corrupted character ('டám') present in the iteration 2 draft that caused the q8 gate failure.
